content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

September 14, 1976

The Honorable Wadie P. Deddeh
Member of Assembly
State Capitol, Room 4111
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-184

Dear Mr. Deddeh:

     This letter is to advise you that at its August meeting the Fair Political Practices Commission instructed the staff to send you this letter in lieu of the formal opinion which you requested (No. 75-184). Since accepting your request for an opinion, the Commission has promulgated regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225 defining the term "expenditure". This regulation would seem to answer your questions and obviate the need for an opinion.

     In your opinion request, you asked the Commission whether holiday greeting cards were subject to the requirements of Government Code Section 84305. Garrett Edmunds of your staff informed the Commission staff that the printing and mailing of the holiday greeting cards would be financed either by your committee or from your personal funds. We have concluded that the procedures set forth in the first sentence of Government Code Section 84305 probably will be applicable only if the cards are financed by your controlled committee.

     Government Code Section 84305 states that no person shall make an expenditure for the purpose of sending a mass mailing the cost of which is reportable pursuant to Chapter 4 unless specific mailing procedures are observed. Government Code section 82041.5 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18241 defined a mass mailing as 200 or more identical or nearly identical pieces of mail sent within a calendar month. Your holiday greeting cards undoubtedly are a mass mailing since your letter indicates that you customarily send approximately 5,000 such cards. Moreover, all payments made by your committee for the purpose of printing or mailing your greeting cards will be "expenditures", since Government Code Section 82025 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225(b) provide that all monetary or nonmonetary payments made by a controlled committee are expenditures. Consequently, payments for your holiday greeting cards which are incurred by your controlled committee will activate the requirements of Government Code Section 84305.

Wadie P. Deddeh
September 14, 1976
Page Two


     If, however, the costs of printing and mailing your
holiday greeting cards are paid by you personally, it is doubtful
that such payments will constitute expenditures.  Pursuant to 2
Cal. Adm. Code Section 18225(b), all monetary or nonmonetary pay-
ments made by a candidate are expenditures, unless it is clear
from all the surrounding circumstances that such payments were
made for personal purposes unrelated to his candidacy or status as
an officeholder. Although at the time you send the cards you
probably will be a candidate within the meaning of Government Code
Section 82007, we think that holiday greeting cards are personal
in nature and, therefore, fall within the personal purposes excep-
tion. The exception would not be applicable, however, if the
cards were used as a vehicle for expressly advocating your nomina-
tion or election. If payments made in connection with sending the
holiday greeting cards are not expenditures, the mailing procedures
set forth in the first sentence of Government Code Section 84305
are not applicable.

     If, after considering the above, you determine that
payments made in connection with your holiday greeting cards are
expenditures, you must send the cards by bulk rate or postage
meter or place the name and street address of the sender on the
outside of the envelope and on one of the inserts in no less than
6-point type. Please find enclosed a copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18435 defining the term "sender". If the sender is your
controlled committee, a post office box number will suffice for
the street address.

     The requirements set forth in the second sentence of
Government Code Section 84305 requiring that a copy of any mass
mailing in support of or in opposition to a state candidate or
state measure be forwarded to the Commission probably is inap-
plicable in your case. Copies of mass mailings must be forwarded
to the Commission only if the mailing expressly advocates your
nomination or election, or, taken as a whole, unambiguously urges
such a result. We assume your holiday greeting cards resemble
those sent by most citizens and thus fall short of this standard.
Accordingly, a copy will not have to be forwarded to the Commission.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Michael Bennett
                                        Executive Director

MB:plh
Enclosures §§ 18225, 18241, 18435

SACRAMENTO ASSEMBLY
STATE CAPITOL, ROOM 4111
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 445-3916

Control No. ___________

Received 9.29.75

Response Due 10-13-75 CALIFORNIA
COMMITTEE
CHAIRMAN
WATS AND PUBLIC EMPLOYEE BENEFITS

DISTRICT OFFICE
619 THIRD AVENUE, SUITE 219
CHULA VISTA, CALIFORNIA 92011
(714) 422-5963
427-7380
GARRY EDMUNDS
ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT

Assembly
California Legislature

WADIE P. DEDDEH
ASSEMBLYMAN, EIGHTIETH DISTRICT
SAN DIEGO COUNTY

September 24, 1975

Fair Campaign Practices Commission
1015 L Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Over the past several years during my term as a California State
Assemblyman it has been my custom to send to friends, supporters
and constituents holiday greeting cards each year in December.

Each year this mailing amounts to approximately 5,000 and contains
the usual and traditional holiday greeting. I would like to
request an opinion of the Commission as to whether or not this
constitutes a political mass mailing as provided for by Proposition
9 with the resulting requirements as to method of mailing and
filing of copy of the mailing with the Commission.

It has been my custom in the past to mail out these cards first
class rate with a first class postage stamp on them and, as I
view this as a personal Christmas greeting, I would prefer to
avoid the impersonal appearance of utilizing either bulk rate
postage indicia or postage meter to mail out these greetings.

I would appreciate your rapid response to this inquiry so that I
might make plans as necessary for the preparation and mailing of
this years holiday greetings.

Very truly yours,

[SIGNATURE]

WADIE P. DEDDEH

WPD:gfs
